title: lora_rx_callback checks lichen_iface==NULL but cannot distinguish init-failure vs never-initialized
description:
In lichen_l2.c lora_rx_callback() (line 352), the NULL check on lichen_iface protects against crashes, but lichen_iface can be NULL for two distinct reasons:
1. Callback invoked before iface_init completed (race during startup)
2. iface_init failed partway through (iface_init_failed=true, but lichen_iface still NULL)

The current warning 'No interface registered' is correct for case 1 but misleading for case 2 - the interface was registered but initialization failed.

Trigger: LoRa packet arrives after iface_init failed (e.g., due to lichen_lora_l2_init failure or EUI-64 fetch failure).

Bad behavior: Log says 'No interface registered' when the real problem is 'interface initialization failed'. Debugging requires correlating with earlier init error logs.

Consider: checking iface_init_failed first and logging a more specific message.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c lines 352-355
